mnemonics: SPJ - Select, Project, Join: The three core operations for querying data.
Think of σ (sigma) as a filter that Sifts data, π (pi) for Picking columns, and ⋈ (bowtie) for Blending tables.
keyPoints: The SELECT operation (σ) is used to choose rows that satisfy a given predicate.
The PROJECT operation (π) is used to select specific columns (attributes) from a relation.
The JOIN operation (⋈) combines tuples from two relations based on a related column between them.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Select operation (σ) with the SQL SELECT statement, which is more analogous to the Project operation (π).
Forgetting that the Project operation (π) removes duplicate tuples by default, as relations are sets.
